name: Collective: investigative journalism on screen
description: In Collective, documentarian Alexander Nanau follows a team of journalists who uncover a far-reaching government conspiracy following a deadly nightclub fire in Romania that kills dozens.

key_work.title: Collective
key_work.creator_credit: Alexander Nanau, Bernard Michaux, Bianca Oana, Hanka Kastelicová, Alexander Nanau Production, Centrul National al Cinematografiei (CNC), HBO Europe, Luxembourg Film Fund, Magnolia Pictures, MDR, Participant, Samsa Film, Sundance Institute and Antoaneta Opris
key_work.brief_description: In the aftermath of a tragic fire in a Romanian club, burn victims begin dying in hospitals from wounds that were not life threatening. A team of investigative journalists move into action uncovering the mass corruption of the health system and of the state institutions. Collective follows journalists, whistle blowers, and authorities alike. An immersive and uncompromising look into a dysfunctional system, exposing corruption, propaganda, and manipulation that nowadays affect not only Romania, but societies around the world.

end.title: Spotlight
end.creator_credit: Tom McCarthy, Wendy Chuck, Blye Pagon Faust, Michael Sugar, Nicole Rocklin, Steve Golin, Anonymous Content, Entertainment One, First Look Media, Open Road Films, Participant, Rocklin / Faust, Topic Studios, Universal Pictures, Stephen H. Carter and Josh Singer
end.headline_credit: Canada and United States of America, 2015

video_links.name: Spotlight | Official Trailer [HD] | Global Road Entertainment

end.brief_description: The true story of how the Boston Globe uncovered the massive scandal of child molestation and cover-up within the local Catholic Archdiocese, shaking the entire Catholic Church to its core.

constellations_other.name: Collective: investigative journalism on screen
constellations_other.description: In Collective, documentarian Alexander Nanau follows a team of journalists who uncover a far-reaching government conspiracy following a deadly nightclub fire in Romania that kills dozens.

constellations_other.name: Uncovering the truth
constellations_other.description: The images of Tank Man during the Tiananmen Square protests in 1989 were captured by photojournalists and shared around the world. The work of journalists has been key to uncovering abuses of power and are rich material for fictional and factual movies and TV.

end.creator_credit_for_label: Tom McCarthy, Wendy Chuck, Blye Pagon Faust, Michael Sugar, Nicole Rocklin, Steve Golin, Anonymous Content, Entertainment One, First Look Media, Open Road Films, Participant, Rocklin / Faust, Topic Studios, Universal Pictures, Stephen H. Carter and Josh Singer

links.description: Although an observational documentary, The Guardian compared Collective to Spotlight due to its exhilarating storyline and high-stakes setting, which led to governmental changes and sweeping reform.

end.title: All the President's Men
end.creator_credit: Alan J. Pakula, Jon Boorstin, Michael Britton, Walter Coblenz, Warner Bros. Pictures, Wildwood Enterprises, George Jenkins, Bob Woodward, Carl Bernstein and William Goldman
end.brief_description: During the 1972 elections, two reporters’ investigation sheds light on the controversial Watergate scandal that compels President Richard Nixon to resign from his post.

links.description: Winning the Best Picture Oscar, Spotlight followed investigative journalists who discovered widespread child sexual abuse in Boston by the Catholic Church. Like All The Presidents’ Men, it was also based on a true story.

end.title: The Pelican brief
end.creator_credit: Alan Pakula, Pieter Jan Brugge and Warner Bros Pictures
end.brief_description: A law student (Julia Roberts) turns her suspicions about the deaths of two Supreme Court Justices into a speculative brief that has the potential to rock the highest levels of government. She and a determined investigative reporter (Denzel Washington) want to expose the scandal to the world, the only challenge is to live to tell the tale. This suspenseful film is based on the best-selling thriller by John Grisham.

constellations_other.name: Collective: investigative journalism on screen

links.description: All The Presidents’ Men dramatised the events surrounding Watergate which saw reporters Woodward and Bernstein break one of the biggest stories of all time. It was directed by Alan J. Pakula, whose film The Pelican Brief also featured an investigative journalist.

end.title: The Girl with the Dragon Tattoo
end.creator_credit: Niels Arden Oplev, Cilla Rörby, Søren Stærmose, Det Danske Filminstitut, Film Capital Stockholm, Film i Väst, Nordisk Film, Nordisk Film & TV Fond, Spiltan Underhållning, Svenska Filminstitutet, SVT, Yellow Bird, ZDF, Niels Sejer, Nikolaj Arcel and Rasmus Heisterberg

video_links.name: THE GIRL WITH THE DRAGON TATTOO - English Trailer

end.brief_description: Swedish thriller based on Stieg Larsson’s novel about a male journalist and a young female hacker. In the opening of the movie, Mikael Blomkvist, a middle-aged publisher for the magazine Millennium, loses a libel case brought by corrupt Swedish industrialist Hans-Erik Wennerström. Nevertheless, he is hired by Henrik Vanger in order to solve a cold case, the disappearance of Vanger’s niece

links.description: The Pelican Brief sees Denzel Washington plays a fictional reporter who uncovers a government conspiracy, not unlike the character of Mikael Blomkvist in another fast-paced thriller: Sweden’s The Girl With The Dragon Tattoo.

links.description: The Girl With The Dragon Tattoo’s intrepid reporter Blomkvist was said to be inspired by the author of the original novel, Steig Larsson. The Swedish journalist spent much of his career uncovering Neo-Nazis and was featured in documentary White Terror.

start.creator_credit: Daniel Schweizer, Annette Pisacane, Matthieu Belghiti and Samir

end.title: Stieg Larsson: The Man Who Played with Fire
end.creator_credit: Henrik Georgsson, Metha Engqvist Griffiths, B-Reel Films, C More Entertainment, Film i Väst, Generalissimo, Nordsvensk Filmunderhållning, TV4, Nathan Grossman and Olof Berglind
end.brief_description: A documentary about the Millennium Trilogy author Stieg Larsson and his pioneering work fighting right-wing extremists and neo-Nazis, an obsession with fatal consequences.

constellations_other.name: Collective: investigative journalism on screen
constellations_other.description: In Collective, documentarian Alexander Nanau follows a team of journalists who uncover a far-reaching government conspiracy following a deadly nightclub fire in Romania that kills dozens.

links.description: Larsson appears in White Terror, which follows journalists as they investigate an extremist network across Europe, Russia and the US. He spent much of his career reporting on the spread of white supremacy, as shown in documentary Die Stieg Larsson Story.
